title: hieracium atratum subsp. rubefactum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. rubefactum is a subspecies of the Hieracium genus, commonly known as the wood hawkweed or red-tinged hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium atratum subsp. rubefactum include its ability to reduce inflammation and pain, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and certain infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and derive its health benefits.
